reasoning: Based on the FRED statistical analysis and expert consensus, the Nominal Broad U.S. Dollar Index has been in a downward trend since early 2025, dropping from ~128 to ~120. Over the past 30 days, the linear trend (slope = -0.014 per day) estimates a low likelihood of exceeding the present value in the near term (P=0.28). While a slight upward bump occurred in the most recent week, broad macroeconomic indicators and an extensive early January poll of currency strategists (Reuters) show overwhelming expectations for continued USD weakness in Q1 2026. This softening is primarily driven by anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts and concerns over Fed independence. Since the forecast requires the index to exceed its January 18th level, these bearish signals provide solid evidence that the likelihood of the index increasing by Jan 25 and Feb 17 is below 0.5. The uncertainty grows as the forecast horizon extends, and given the prevailing downward trend, the chance of the index maintaining an elevated level further out on Feb 17 is lower than on Jan 25. Thus, I estimate a 42% probability for Jan 25, 2026, and a 38% probability for Feb 17, 2026.
